name: requirements-analysis-expert
description: Provides guidance and opinions on requirements analysis approaches and techniques

requirements_gathering_approach.title: Stakeholder Interview vs Assumption-Based Design
requirements_gathering_approach.description: How to gather requirements before architecture design
requirements_gathering_approach.rationale: Assumption-based design leads to fundamental architectural errors that are expensive to fix. Stakeholder interviews take 1-2 hours but prevent weeks of rework.

when_not_applicable: Well-documented existing systems with zero changes
Pure code refactoring with no business logic changes

options.name: Stakeholder Interview First (RECOMMENDED)
pros: Identifies all user types and their actual needs
Catches illogical assumptions early (e.g., external users in internal JIRA)
1-2 hours investment prevents weeks of rework
cons: Stakeholders may not be immediately available
options.when_to_use: Always, for any architecture project with unclear requirements

options.name: Assumption-Based Design (NOT RECOMMENDED)
cons: May design for wrong users or wrong problem
Expensive rework when assumptions are wrong
May get you fired if presented to executives
options.when_to_use: Never, unless you enjoy career-limiting mistakes

decision_factors.guidance: More complex = more critical to interview stakeholders first
Unclear who uses what systems = mandatory stakeholder interview
More integrations = more critical to understand business justification for each
High visibility = zero tolerance for fundamental errors

requirements_gathering_approach.recommendation: ALWAYS conduct stakeholder interviews before architecture design. The 1-2 hour investment prevents career-limiting mistakes.

validation_before_design.title: Present User-System Matrix for Approval vs Proceed with Assumptions
validation_before_design.description: Whether to validate user-to-system mapping before generating architecture diagrams
validation_before_design.rationale: A simple user-to-system matrix takes 5 minutes to create and catches 90% of architectural errors before any design work begins

when_applicable: Any architecture with unclear user access patterns
when_not_applicable: Well-documented existing system with no changes

options.name: Present User-System Matrix for Approval (MANDATORY)
pros: Forces explicit thinking about who uses what
Stakeholder reviews and catches illogical mappings
Prevents generating 9 documents with fundamental errors
options.when_to_use: ALWAYS, before any architecture diagram or documentation

options.name: Proceed with Assumptions (NEVER DO THIS)
pros: None. Seriously, there are no pros.
cons: May show external users in internal JIRA (career-limiting)
Stakeholder loses confidence in your work
options.when_to_use: Never. This is the path to unemployment.

decision_factors.guidance: 5 minutes to create matrix vs weeks to fix errors
Shows diligence and prevents 'how did you miss this?' questions

validation_before_design.recommendation: ALWAYS create and present user-system matrix for approval before generating any architecture diagrams or documentation.

external_user_access_validation.title: External Users and Internal System Access
external_user_access_validation.description: When (if ever) external users should access internal systems like JIRA, internal CRM, internal project management tools
external_user_access_validation.rationale: External users accessing internal systems is almost always an architectural error unless there is explicit, documented business justification (e.g., vendor portal)

when_applicable: Any architecture with external users (customers, partners)
Architectures integrating internal systems (JIRA, internal CRM, admin tools)
when_not_applicable: Pure internal architecture with no external users

options.name: External Users Access Internal Systems (RARE, NEEDS JUSTIFICATION)
pros: May be justified for vendor portals
May be justified for partner integrations with specific business cases
cons: Complexity (managing external user permissions in internal systems)
options.when_to_use: Only with explicit business justification and security review (e.g., vendor portal for suppliers)

options.name: External Users Access External-Facing Systems Only (CORRECT 99% OF TIME)

decision_factors.guidance: External users in internal systems = high security risk
Internal system licenses for external users are expensive

external_user_access_validation.recommendation: External users should ONLY access external-facing systems (self-service portals, public APIs). If architecture shows external users in JIRA, internal CRM, or internal tools, STOP and validate business justification.
